flashcards.front: What is the primary focus of unit testing?
flashcards.back: Verifying individual components/modules in isolation using stubs/mocks.
Big Bang, Top-down, Bottom-up, and Sandwich (hybrid) approaches.
Entire system compliance with specified requirements in production-like environment.
Re-testing modified software to ensure existing functionality remains unaffected.
Collection of test drivers/stubs used to support integration testing.

flashcards.front: What's the difference between stub and driver?
flashcards.back: Stub simulates called components, driver simulates calling components.
Preliminary test to check basic functionality before detailed testing.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of alpha testing?
flashcards.back: Internal testing by development team in lab environment.

flashcards.front: What does V-model emphasize about testing?
flashcards.back: Testing activities parallel to development phases.
Subset of integration testing focusing on module interactions.

flashcards.front: What is the main challenge in big bang integration?
flashcards.back: Difficulty isolating faults due to simultaneous integration.
